name: holocentromere_architecture
description: Architecture record for the C. elegans holocentromere. Holocentric chromosomes lack a localised centromere; instead the CENP-A homolog HCP-3 is deposited at ~700 small discrete sites along the length of each chromosome. The targeting determinant is chromatin-state (transcription anti-correlation, H3K9me-poor euchromatin) rather than a satellite consensus. Repeat content at these sites is not elevated above genomic background, so read mapping does not require special multi-mapping flags. Holocentromere awareness only matters for downstream chromatin / centromere-specific analyses (CenH3 ChIP-seq, kinetochore protein localisation).

architecture.internal_structure: ~700 HCP-3 sites per chromosome, median 1.5 kb wide, irregularly spaced; no defining satellite repeat
